title: Wire the 2D renderer into scene-driven fixtures
description: Move rendering validation from synthetic framebuffer tests to scene-driven golden fixtures. Acceptance criteria: at least one .tscn scene produces golden-compared output and validates camera, transforms, ordering, texture draws, and visibility end to end.

title: Expand apps/godot probes for API and resource validation
description: Extend apps/godot with API and resource probes so the lab can emit machine-readable outputs for class APIs, resources, and lifecycle assumptions. Acceptance: apps/godot emits reproducible probe output for at least one API surface and one resource fixture.

title: Add deterministic physics trace goldens
description: Capture and compare deterministic physics traces for one representative scene. Acceptance: a checked-in golden trace exists under fixtures/golden/physics and tests compare Patina output against it with documented thresholds.

title: Replace manual parity plumbing with generated oracle artifacts
description: Move parity tests to generated upstream-backed goldens. Acceptance criteria: oracle tests consume fixtures from fixtures/golden, each test states the observable contract it compares, and stale/missing goldens fail clearly.

title: Add repeated-load cache deduplication regression tests
description: Add integration tests that prove repeated loads reuse cached resources and preserve stable identities. Acceptance: cache semantics are covered by regression tests for direct loads and ext-resource reuse.

title: Document engine input contract for examples and tests
description: Write down the engine-owned input contract so examples, tests, and future frontends use one model. Acceptance: one doc defines pressed, just-pressed, mouse, and action-map semantics for Patina runtime consumers.

title: Map space shooter demo to oracle and render fixture targets
description: Document the space shooter demo as a feeder for oracle/runtime/render fixtures instead of proof of completion. Acceptance: shooter example points to explicit fixture targets and parity gaps.

title: Map each example to a measurable fixture target
description: Treat examples as fixture feeders instead of proof of completion by documenting their target parity fixtures and reducing duplicated orchestration. Acceptance: each maintained example points to a measurable fixture or compatibility target.

title: Remove example-local loop orchestration from platformer and shooter demos
description: Reduce demo-specific runtime orchestration in platformer and shooter examples so they consume the engine-owned loop/input flow. Acceptance: demo code no longer recreates the core process/physics/render contract.

title: Probe ClassDB and node API signatures from apps/godot
description: Use apps/godot to emit machine-readable ClassDB or node API signature data for one representative set of runtime classes. Acceptance: probe output is reproducible and checked by at least one regression path.

title: Compare Patina frame traces against upstream frame-trace goldens
description: Extend engine-rs/tests/oracle_regression_test.rs to compare Patina --trace-frames output against upstream frame_trace data for at least one scripted fixture. Acceptance: regression tests fail clearly on per-frame property evolution drift and cover the scripted motion path.

title: Probe resource metadata and roundtrip behavior from apps/godot
description: Use apps/godot to capture resource metadata and roundtrip assumptions for one representative resource fixture. Acceptance: probe output covers at least one resource roundtrip and feeds the oracle path.

title: Add mouse position and button routing to input snapshots
description: Extend the engine input snapshot path to include mouse position and button state. Acceptance: mouse basics are exposed through runtime APIs and covered by tests.

title: Add global lifecycle and signal ordering trace parity
description: Capture one ordered runtime event stream for notifications, lifecycle callbacks, and emitted signals, then compare it against oracle output. Acceptance: trace output is deterministic and at least one oracle regression compares total ordering directly.

title: Classify editor example as maintenance-only workflow support
description: Reframe the editor example as maintenance-only workflow support until runtime parity exits are met. Acceptance: docs and backlog clearly classify the editor example outside active milestone progress.

title: Reclassify 3D-adjacent scaffolding out of 2D milestone reporting
description: Update milestone and compatibility reporting so unfinished 3D-adjacent scaffolding is not counted as 2D progress. Acceptance: docs clearly separate deferred 3D work from the active 2D milestone.

title: Compare lifecycle notification traces against oracle output
description: Extend oracle regression coverage to compare notification or lifecycle traces against oracle output for at least one fixture. Acceptance: enter, ready, process, physics, and exit ordering are checked directly.

title: Compare runtime signal traces against oracle trace output
description: Extend oracle regression coverage to compare runtime signal trace output against oracle signal traces for at least one scripted fixture. Acceptance: ordering, emitter path, and argument payloads are checked in a regression test.

title: Cover keyboard action snapshots through engine input API
description: Exercise action-map and key-state snapshots through the engine-owned input API. Acceptance: keyboard pressed and just-pressed behavior are covered by tests instead of example-local code.

title: Generate upstream frame-trace golden for test_scripts
description: Run tools/oracle/run_fixture.gd against fixtures/scenes/test_scripts.tscn with the pinned upstream Godot and check in a full oracle capture containing frame_trace. Acceptance: fixtures/oracle_outputs/test_scripts.json is regenerated from upstream, includes frame_trace, and records the current upstream pin metadata.

title: Make frame processing semantics match Godot contracts
description: Align process, physics_process, pause, and fixed-step behavior with Godot. Acceptance criteria: frame sequencing is specified and tested, and oracle fixtures validate frame-by-frame property evolution rather than only end-state values.

title: Break winit backend responsibilities into runtime-owned services
description: Identify and split display, window, and input responsibilities that still live in the backend/example layer instead of engine-owned runtime services. Acceptance: a concrete file-level split is documented and at least one responsibility moves behind a runtime-facing API.

title: Expose engine-owned input snapshot and routing API
description: Move input handling behind a stable engine-facing API used by runtime code instead of ad hoc example logic. Acceptance: keyboard and mouse basics are available through engine APIs and covered by tests.

title: Gate new gdeditor feature work behind runtime parity exits
description: Codify the project rule that new editor feature expansion stays blocked until runtime parity exits are met. Acceptance: docs and workflow guidance clearly gate editor feature work behind runtime milestones.

title: Pin upstream Godot and record oracle version
description: Add the upstream Godot pin as the authoritative oracle source. Acceptance criteria: upstream is pinned, TEST_ORACLE.md records version/commit/date, and repo setup docs explain how to sync/update the pin.

title: Advance gdphysics2d from MainLoop fixed-step frames
description: Step gdphysics2d from the engine fixed timestep instead of isolated calls. Acceptance: main loop fixed-step processing advances physics deterministically and is covered by frame-level tests.

title: Add maintenance-only reliability checks for editor server smoke paths
description: Keep editor server work bounded to reliability by adding smoke/regression checks without expanding feature scope. Acceptance: maintenance tests cover startup/basic transport reliability and are clearly marked non-milestone work.

title: Integrate resource UID and cache behavior into loader paths
description: Make resource loading behave like one coherent Godot-style system across res:// paths, ext-resources, subresources, and repeated loads. Acceptance: integration tests prove stable identities, deduplication, and UID/path resolution across repeated loads.

title: Separate editor server stability work from editor feature backlog
description: Split editor server reliability work from editor feature work so runtime parity reporting is not diluted. Acceptance: docs and backlog explicitly classify server stability as maintenance and editor features as deferred.

title: Normalize display and window state flow through gdplatform
description: Make display and window state flow through gdplatform rather than example-local orchestration. Acceptance: tests or integration coverage prove a stable display/window state path used by runtime code.

title: Create tools/oracle capture tooling
description: Build the scene/property/signal/notification/resource/render/physics oracle entrypoints. Acceptance criteria: each capture type in TEST_ORACLE.md has a concrete tool entrypoint and at least one scene and one resource fixture can generate golden outputs.

title: Measure one end-to-end 2D vertical slice from fixtures
description: Pick one representative 2D slice and measure it end to end with render and physics evidence, then update status docs from the results. Acceptance: one fixture set compares against render and physics goldens and the dashboard/matrix report measured status.

title: Split compatibility docs into measured status versus deferred scope
description: Adjust compatibility and milestone docs so measured status is separated from deferred scope and scaffolding. Acceptance: COMPAT_* and milestone docs distinguish verified coverage from planned/deferred work.

title: Sync Node2D body nodes with gdphysics2d world state
description: Wire scene nodes such as CharacterBody2D and StaticBody2D into gdphysics2d registration and state sync. Acceptance: body creation, transform sync, and removal are covered by runtime tests.

title: Resolve res:// and UID lookups through one loader path
description: Unify res:// path loading and UID lookup so both resolve through one coherent loader path. Acceptance: tests prove equivalent resolution and stable identities for repeated loads.

title: Label editor test coverage as maintenance-only until runtime exits
description: Audit editor tests and document which ones are maintenance coverage versus deferred feature expansion. Acceptance: editor test/docs language no longer implies editor milestone progress beyond maintenance work.

title: Map platformer demo to physics and render fixture targets
description: Document the platformer demo as a feeder for concrete physics/render fixtures instead of a completion signal. Acceptance: platformer example points to explicit fixture targets and remaining gaps.

title: Add explicit runtime-parity gate to AGENTS and flywheel docs
description: Record in AGENTS.md and flywheel workflow docs that new editor feature work is blocked until runtime parity exits are met. Acceptance: contributor guidance explicitly routes editor requests behind runtime milestones.

title: Stand up apps/godot GDExtension compatibility lab
description: Create the missing Godot lab for Rust-in-Godot contract validation. Acceptance criteria: apps/godot builds as a GDExtension project, Godot loads it, and smoke probes emit machine-readable outputs for scene, resource, and signal behavior.

title: Connect gdphysics2d to scene nodes and fixed-step runtime
description: Integrate gdphysics2d with scene nodes and the fixed-step runtime so body state advances through the engine loop instead of isolated tests. Acceptance: at least one body fixture advances through fixed-step simulation with node transforms and physics state kept in sync.

title: Finish scene-aware signal dispatch parity
description: Replace remaining closure-oriented signal behavior with scene-aware dispatch and add parity tests for duplicate connections, disconnect, argument passing, and lifecycle-triggered emissions. Acceptance: cross-node signal routing behaves consistently under runtime tests and fixture coverage.

title: Add physics_playground golden trace fixture
description: Create a deterministic golden trace for physics_playground and compare Patina output against it. Acceptance: one checked-in golden trace exists with a regression test and documented tolerances.

title: Add input-map loading and action binding coverage
description: Add input map loading from project.godot format and action binding coverage. Acceptance: InputMap can be loaded from project.godot format and bindings resolve correctly.

title: Expand notification coverage beyond lifecycle basics
description: Add tests for non-lifecycle notifications: DRAW, PAUSED, UNPAUSED, MOVED_IN_PARENT, VISIBILITY_CHANGED, TRANSFORM_CHANGED. Acceptance: at least 5 non-lifecycle notification types are verified.

title: Add collision shape registration and overlap coverage
description: Register collision shapes on physics bodies from scene nodes. Test overlap detection.

title: Add CharacterBody2D and StaticBody2D behavior fixtures
description: Create fixture scenes with CharacterBody2D + StaticBody2D, verify they behave correctly.
